MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Dec-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CLASSIC SHUTTLE SERVICES, INC., XIONGYING CHEN's MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE (Code Civ. Proc. section 395(a) and 397(c))

Defendants' motion to change venue to San Mateo County is denied. CCP 397(c) permits transfer from one proper county to another proper county when "the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice would be promoted by the change." The burden is on the party seeking a change of venue to prove 1) that the convenience of witnesses and 2) that the ends of justice will be promoted by a change. (Corfee v. Southern California Edison Co. (1962) 202 Cal.App.2d 473, 477.) To show the convenience of witnesses, the moving party "must do through affidavits which contain something more than generalities and conclusions. Affidavits or declarations in support of the motion must set forth the names of the witnesses, the nature of the testimony expected from each, and the reasons why the attendance of each would be inconvenient." (Corfee, 202 Cal.App.2d, at 477.) Defendants argue that the convenience of witnesses here is that "the residence of the witnesses is in the county to which the transfer of the cause of action is requested." (Defs.' Mot. 6:22-23.) However, the "convenience of the witnesses in attending the trial is the controlling element in connection with such a motion, rather than the place of their actual residence." (Dick v. La Madrid (1947) 82 Cal.App.2d 450, 452.) It does not matter that witnesses live in a different county if the moving party does not show that a change would actually be more convenient. (See Id. [holding in a motion to change venue from Kern to LA County that alleging that testifying Los Angeles police officers were Los Angeles officers was insufficient without an actual showing of inconvenience because it is "possible, however improbable it may be, that they would welcome an opportunity to leave Los Angeles for a short time."].)

Defendants fail to show any actual inconvenience, beyond claims that the witnesses live or work in San Mateo County. Mr. Misa testifies that it "would be more convenient . . . to testify in Redwood City" not that it would be inconvenient to testify in San Francisco. (Cholakian Decl., Ex. E, Misa Depo. 36:12-15; see Corfee, 202 Cal.App.2d at 477 [holding that affidavits must show why the attendance of witnesses "would be inconvenient."].) Officer Chinca testifies that the difference between San Francisco and Redwood City is "kind of a toss-up." (Cholakian Decl., Ex. F, Chinca Depo. 35:17.) Officer Smithmatungol testified that location does not "really matter . . . Either/or works for me." Rose Decl., Ex. C, Smithmatungol Depo. 22:14-17.)

Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/HEK)
